reason: Both answers correctly diagnose alert noise (not rotation size) as the core problem, give nearly identical concrete steps: audit alerts, page only on actionable/urgent symptoms, route non-urgent items to a queue or business hours, track a pages-per-shift metric, and review noise periodically. Both correctly conclude the six-person weekly rotation is fine once paging is fixed, and follow-the-sun is not the answer. Both omit the point about reducing sprint commitment during on-call weeks. Minor differences (A cites an SRE benchmark; B is slightly more concise with a memorable closing line) roughly cancel out, making this a genuine tie in quality.
